column4: Given a user is in the RHOAI dashboard, when they access the role creation feature from the Roles tab within a Data Science Project, then they are presented with a form-based UI for defining custom roles.
column6: Roles tab is accessible within Data Science Project. Clicking Create role presents a form-based UI with metadata fields and rules editor.

column4: Given a user is creating a custom role, when they need to specify permissions, then they can select API groups, resources, and verbs (permissions) without manual typing.
column6: API group and resource inputs accept text. Verb selection uses toggle buttons — no manual typing required for permission verbs.

column4: Given a user is creating a role, when they want to start from a common pattern, then they can select templates shipped OOTB and the form is pre-populated with the template's rules, which they can then edit.
column6: Template selector dropdown shows OOTB templates (Workbench User etc.). Selecting a template pre-populates the rules table with multiple rules that can be edited.

column4: Given a user has filled out the role creation form, when they submit or review their inputs, then the UI validates the inputs to ensure the resulting RBAC is valid within the cluster, measured by invalid role names being caught by the existing ODH name validation component and Kubernetes API server errors being surfaced to the user.
column6: Submit button is correctly disabled when role name is empty (validation works). However success alert after submission was not captured — the alert auto-dismisses after 1.5s before being verified.
column8: Increase success alert display duration or add persistent validation feedback that remains visible after submission

column4: Given the user is viewing the role list view on the Roles tab, when they select a role to view, then the corresponding YAML is displayed in read-only mode.
column6: Clicking View YAML on an existing role opens a modal displaying the full Role YAML definition in read-only CodeBlock format.

column4: Given the user created a custom role through the form, then the Role resource in Kubernetes has the opendatahub.io/dashboard=true label present on it, measured by kubectl get role <name> -n <namespace> -o yaml showing the label.
column6: YAML preview in the create form shows opendatahub.io/dashboard: true label in the metadata section for all created roles.

column5: Complete role creation flow works end-to-end: navigate to Roles tab, open form, select template, fill fields, preview YAML, submit.
Single-user feature (role creation within a project). No cross-role handoff tested.
Template selector bridges novice-expert gap. Junior can use templates; senior can customize rules directly.
Submit button disabled state provides validation feedback. YAML preview updates live. Success alert auto-dismisses too quickly.
K8s terminology (API groups, verbs) exposed directly. Templates provide friendliness layer but form fields remain technical.
Form structure matches RBAC mental model for admins. Junior users struggle with K8s-native terminology.
PatternFly components provide WCAG 2.1 AA baseline. Form labels, aria-labels present. Verb toggle buttons use color-only differentiation.
